evidence.title: Notamify raw OIIX A1010/26 and A1015/26 NOTAM mirror
evidence.source_description: Captured text from public Notamify OIIX NOTAM pages opened during defendant arbitration investigation; Notamify is an unofficial NOTAM mirror, not the original Iranian AIS publication.
evidence.relevance: Provides the exact mirrored NOTAM wording and Q code/category for A1010/26 and replacement A1015/26. Relevant to whether the instrument was an aerodrome/airport-operations closure rather than a broad airspace closure, and to conflicts with secondary reports about which airports remained operational.

evidence.title: EASA CZIB 2026-03-R13 Middle East airspace bulletin
evidence.source_description: Captured text from official EASA conflict zone information bulletin page opened during arbitration investigation.
evidence.relevance: Official aviation authority context for non-weather security risk, affected Iranian airspace, and temporary airspace closures/restrictions by states during the conflict; corroborates that the relevant aviation disruptions were conflict/security related, not weather.

evidence.title: RFE/RL live report: Airspace Closed Over Western Iran, May 23 2026
evidence.source_description: Captured page text from Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty live blog item opened during arbitration investigation; item timestamped 10:03 23.5.2026.
evidence.relevance: Independent contemporaneous reporting that Iran's Civil Aviation Organization announced temporary restrictions over western airspace, severe passenger-flight restrictions, eight airports permitted, daylight-only operations, and individual CAA permits.

evidence.title: Indian Express report on Iran western airspace NOTAM, May 23 2026
evidence.source_description: Captured page text from Indian Express article opened during arbitration investigation; article dated May 23, 2026.
evidence.relevance: Reports the alleged Iranian Civil Aviation Authority NOTAM OIIX A1010/26, effective May 22-May 25, closing western Tehran FIR and limiting operations to eight airports with daylight-only and per-flight approval restrictions.

evidence.title: SafeAirspace Iran country page, June 2026 operational context
evidence.source_description: Captured text from SafeAirspace Iran country risk page opened during arbitration investigation.
evidence.relevance: Aviation-risk context showing OIIX/Tehran FIR status, partial reopening/western closure in June 2026, and that major airspace restrictions were security-related rather than weather-related; also useful for adverse scope because it distinguishes partial reopening from full normal operations.
